title: Living in Boulder vlog 12 - 14 - 18: #17 100 Miles a day. Can't get enough of this biking

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder. It's 8.29 p.m. Time just keeps creeping up on me. I'm fixing to do
Another bike ride. I just kind of tried to have some dinner. It's hard for me to eat though.
My big problem is being completely out of shape and all of a sudden I'm riding all the time.
The problem with that is that my stomach has trouble digesting lots of food. I can't eat a lot.
It's kind of like I've got a Tesla Model 3 just like everybody else. Maybe not a roadster,
But I'm a solid rider. The problem is I'm not using a good charge cable. My charge cable is
Really thin and so it takes a whole day to recharge my battery. Whereas a normal person
Could probably charge it up in three hours or whatever. Because, yeah, I'd just get sick to
My stomach and I would barf, you know. And so that actually would, that takes away from your
Strength probably on the bike, but I don't know. But, you know, it's a whole balanced system,
You know. So maybe I wouldn't even have it anyway. I rode yesterday. It was the second day in a row
Where I did sub six. I didn't think I was going to do sub six. I felt bad. I was having to get off
The bike a bunch of times because I forgot stuff. I forgot. I didn't fill up enough bottles. Day before
Yesterday, I did the whole ride and 552 and I think I only went through four bottles. So for some reason
Last night I only filled up four bottles. And so then I ran out and I had an hour of riding to go.
So I had to go and fill up another bottle and put my energy drink in it. And then that bottle was
Empty by the end of the ride. So I went through five solid bottles. No, it was empty before the end
Of the ride. And then I had to drink a bunch of fruit juice that I had. So it was like six bottles.
So I was a water guzzler yesterday. I think like two more bottles. Maybe it was because I started
The ride more dehydrated though. But it's just interesting how stuff fluctuates. But I ended up
Being able to do the whole ride in under six hours. So, you know, my general strength, even if I'm
Feeling wiped out, my general bike efficiency is better. And I, so I can maintain a faster speed and
Get done on under an hour, six hours. Um, uh, one of my big problems, I think I forgot to talk about
It though. In the beginning was my hips, the tendons in my hips were hurting and feeling like they were
Getting stretched out of their sockets and stuff. Um, and, uh, like all over my hip and, but I don't
Really feel that anymore. I did feel it yesterday though. It's still happening, but it's not as bad.
It's getting better. And once my hip muscles and tendons mainly, I think firm up, that'll make me,
Uh, I'll be able to ride faster. It'll just give me more strength. Um, and, uh, my legs are sore,
But they're not, um, they're not, I don't think they're more sore than they have been throughout the
Ride. You know, when I walk upstairs, I'm sore. They're burned, but they don't burn really bad.
Um, and they just pretty much steady like that. So this, uh, this thing where you ride for six
Hours and then you sleep for 18 hours is actually, um, a good way, a good, it provides enough sleep to
Keep piling on the six hours, six hours, 100 miles a day without taking a break, a rest day. Um, my mom
Wants me to go Christmas shopping with her tomorrow and she says she's going to, and then she says I
Have to watch a movie with her, Mary Queen of Scots movie in the theater. So, and she says she's going
To pick me up at 10 in the morning. So, and I usually have insomnia until about, um, I'm usually up until
Like eight or nine. It's like eight 30 this morning. So I'll be just falling asleep. So we'll see how that
Works. I don't know if I'll be able to ride tomorrow. Um, for me to be, one thing that I realized in my
Old age and probably most endurance athletes, all of the successful ones probably realized this early
On. It just took me fucking 25 years to realize it. Uh, the most important thing if you want to be a
Successful endurance athlete is you have to cut out the distractions. You have to have no distractions and
You have to have, and if you, and if you're surrounded by anybody, they have to be encouraging
You to do what you're doing. Like you have to be in a world of endurance athleticism. You can't have
People like my mom or like, you're going to take the day off today. God damn it. You're doing it too
Much, you know? So, but I'm, I think my mom has figured it out though too. She's just that it's
Christmas, you know? Um, but, uh, but yeah, I'm super, uh, um, super, super, super, I have the same
Exact philosophy about working, riding my bike now that I learned from working on my website and that
I kind of fell into naturally from working on my website where you're obsessed with it. And that's
Like all you do and everything else revolves around it. So anyways, I got up and I was hungry
Because I slept all day and I had an avocado and then I had a, uh, oh, and I cleaned my fish tank for
Like an hour. Um, I got these fish, these bright red fish. I don't know what they are, but I got four
Of them and then they multiplied into like 20 or something, 20 or 30. And I noticed one was feeling
Sick. And so he kind of waddled his way over to the heater. That's what the fish do when they die.
They go and hang out at the heater. And then I saw that he died. And then I saw he's kind of
Starting to decompose. And I washed my fish tank and I looked back there where the body was and
There was nothing there. So I think the fish eat there. The fish that I have, they don't float
When they die. They sink. And then the other ones eat, they're dead. And then I saw there's two baby
Ones. So the fish that I have, they'd like populate the shit out of my tank and then they die, but you
Don't have to see that they die because they just fall to the bottom and get eaten. And then they have
Babies. It's a cool kind of fish to have, man. It's like the everlasting fish. Fish that
Never go away because they keep breeding. And then they die and eat themselves. Eat each
Other. So anyway, I'll try to mention it tomorrow, what fish that is. But yeah, yesterday I rode
And listened to music. Today, that's what I mean I'm going to do today, more listening to music.
Yesterday, I was super fired up. I've been, for the last few years since I moved to Boulder
And throughout my life, I guess to an extent, I always expected that I was going to be a
Professional athlete at one point in my life. I never expected that it would take until I'm
Fucking 45 years old. So for the last like three years, I've been thinking about it more
And more and more and more until I'm thinking about it all the time until I completely lost
My mind. And so when I'm riding my bike, it's basically just pure emotion that I'm going
Off of. I'm just like, ah, yes, ah, like I'm, like I was doing, I was like, ah, God damn it.
Ah, like just yelling just for no reason because I was freaked out about not exercising. And I
Do that riding now. Only when I do that riding now, I'm like, ah, I'm like, oh wait, but I am
Riding. Okay, this is it. So it's awesome. I've been feeling good. You know, towards the
Beginning of the ride, I feel groggy. That's the worst part. When you feel groggy when you're
Riding because you feel like something's wrong, like it's going to get worse and then you're
Going to maybe even like die or something. Have a heart attack and die. But, ah, but I
Have these drinks that have caffeine in them. I think my energy gel has caffeine and you just
Get the blood flowing and, you know, it's like you got to warm up a Lamborghini, you
Know, where you can take it, open it up. Just get the blood flowing and after an hour of
That, you wake up and once you're up, you're there and we can do the rest of it. You know,
The only other problem after that would be like if you have super sore legs, like your tendons
Just seize up on you and pain or if your joints break. But yeah, I haven't been having that
Problem. So just listen to music. Um, yeah, so I didn't really eat much today.
When I was done with my ride, I went into the store and I got, um, chips and salsa with
Beans and had some of that and some like, um, seaweed flakes and stuff. So I'm, I'm, I'm
Feeding my body as much as my body can be fed. I have a small stomach. Um, and my appetite
Hasn't kicked in. When my appetite kicks in, I'm going to fucking know it. Um, I've gotten
Myself into shape. I've, I've been in and out of shape dozens of times in my life because
I, because I'm by nature. I want to exercise, but I'm always was stupid and allow myself to
Get in situations where I wasn't allowed to, like going to college and stuff. And, um, so
I know I'm very experienced with not riding. And then all of a sudden you're riding like
Bike touring and stuff for like a month. And I take it maybe a month, maybe a little bit
Over and then I'd stop. But, um, so I know what it's like to ride until your appetite is going.
And yeah, it takes about three weeks. And like, it seems like it happens all at once. Like,
Like you're hanging out and then all of a sudden you're hungry. You have the stomach
Pangs and you're like, but wait, I don't know. I'm not supposed to have stomach pangs now. I just
Ate. You know what I mean? That's when your appetite kicks in. That's when you have to start
Eating. That's when you can eat more and you have to start eating more. But that's also when you
Get stronger. That's when your leg speed kicks in and stuff. So that hasn't happened to me
Yet. Um, predictions for the day. I really don't know, man. I mean, it's, I guess it's
Possible I could do another sub six hour today, today, but, um, I feel kind of groggy. Um,
You know, if I organize myself, I didn't manage the time very well. I got off the bike too many
Times. If I organize that, I could do it, but I'm not going to try to make it happen. Um, it
Wouldn't surprise me at all if I'm after six, but you know, as you, as my legs get more
Efficient, you just naturally ride faster. So, you know, whatever, we'll see what happens.
I'm going to, Oh, one cool thing about yesterday was I pretty much spent the entire ride. You
Know, I was taking breaks, but pretty much the entire ride organizing files on my computer,
You know, and then I organized my files for like another six hours after that. So it was like
12 hours of files organization on my computer. So that was cool. I'm working and writing at
The same time. Okay. That's enough babbling. I hope this, this actually recorded. How can
I get my, how can I get my program back? My program, um, my computer's frozen. Let's hit
Escape. All right. And it recorded the whole thing. This is my old Mac. My old Mac hasn't
Dropped a recording yet. I think on, um, on a quick time. It's my new Mac that does that.
So maybe I should do my new legs on my old Mac. I also want to figure out how to do it
On my computer, my cell phone. Uh, I'll try to do that tomorrow, but I sleep all day long.
Okay. Peace out.
